title: pittosporum viridiflorum
intro: Pittosporum viridiflorum, also known as the Milk Tree or Cheesefruit, is a tree native to South Africa, known for its fragrant, greenish-white flowers and small, edible fruit. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Pittosporum viridiflorum are said to include reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant may include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential side effects.
